audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for pericope adulterae as later textual tradition, light of world, testimony/Father, lifting up, truth/free, ancestry dispute, adversarial father imagery, glory, Abraham, and ego eimi climax.

textual_note: John 7:53-8:11 is widely recognized as a later textual tradition absent from many early manuscripts. It is included here bracketed as a received tradition, not treated as part of the earliest recoverable Johannine text.

verses.restored: [[But Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.]]
[[At early dawn he came again into the temple, and all the people were coming to him; sitting down, he taught them.]]
[[The scribes and Pharisees bring a woman caught in adultery, and standing her in the middle,]]
[[they say to him, Teacher, this woman has been caught in the act of adultery.]]
[[In the Law, Moses commanded us to stone such women. What then do you say?]]
[[They said this testing him, so that they might have something to accuse him. But Jesus bent down and wrote with his finger on the ground.]]
[[As they continued questioning him, he straightened up and said to them, Let the sinless one among you be first to cast a stone at her.]]
[[Again he bent down and wrote on the ground.]]
[[Hearing this, they went out one by one, beginning from the elders, and he was left alone, with the woman in the middle.]]
[[Jesus straightened up and said to her, Woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you?]]
[[She said, No one, Lord. Jesus said, Neither do I condemn you; go, and from now on do not continue in misalignment.]]
Again therefore Jesus spoke to them, saying, I am the light of the world; the one following me will certainly not walk in darkness but will have the light of life.
The Pharisees therefore said to him, You bear witness concerning yourself; your witness is not true/valid.

Jesus answered and said to them, Even if I bear witness concerning myself, my witness is true, because I know from where I came and where I am going; but you do not know from where I come or where I am going.
You judge according to ordinary human appearance; I judge no one in that way.
But even if I judge, my judgment is true, because I am not alone, but I and the Father who sent me.
In your Law it has been written that the witness of two humans is true.
I am the one bearing witness concerning myself, and the Father who sent me bears witness concerning me.
They were saying to him, Where is your Father? Jesus answered, You know neither me nor my Father; if you knew me, you would know my Father also.
These words he spoke in the treasury, teaching in the temple, and no one seized him, because his hour had not yet come.
He said again to them, I am going away, and you will seek me, and in your sin/misalignment you will die; where I am going, you are not able to come.
The Judeans therefore said, Will he kill himself, because he says, Where I am going, you are not able to come?
He said to them, You are from below; I am from above. You are from this world; I am not from this world.
Therefore I said to you that you will die in your sins/misalignments; for unless you trust that I am, you will die in your sins/misalignments.
They were saying to him, Who are you? Jesus said to them, What I have been saying to you from the beginning.
I have many things to speak and judge concerning you, but the One who sent me is true, and what I heard from him, these things I speak into the world.
They did not know that he was speaking to them of the Father.
Jesus therefore said, When you lift up the Son of Humanity, then you will know that I am, and I do nothing from myself, but just as the Father taught me, these things I speak.
The One who sent me is with me; he has not left me alone, because I always do the things pleasing to him.
As he was speaking these things, many trusted into him.
Jesus therefore said to the Judeans who had trusted him, If you remain in my word, you are truly my disciples.
You will know the truth, and the truth will free you.
They answered him, We are seed of Abraham and have never been enslaved to anyone; how do you say, You will become free?
Jesus answered them, Amen, amen, I say to you: everyone practicing sin/misalignment is enslaved to sin/misalignment.
The enslaved one does not remain in the house into the age; the son remains into the age.
If therefore the Son frees you, you will truly be free.
I know that you are seed of Abraham; yet you seek to kill me, because my word has no room in you.
What I have seen with the Father, I speak; and you therefore do what you heard from your father.
They answered and said to him, Our father is Abraham. Jesus says to them, If you are children of Abraham, do the works of Abraham.
But now you seek to kill me, a human who has spoken to you the truth that I heard from the Divine. This Abraham did not do.
You do the works of your father. They said to him, We were not born from sexual immorality; we have one Father, the Divine.
Jesus said to them, If the Divine were your Father, you would love me, for I came out and have come from the Divine; for I have not come from myself, but that One sent me.
Why do you not know my speech? Because you are not able to hear my word.

You are from your father, the adversarial one, and you desire to do the desires of your father. That one was a human-killer from the beginning and has not stood in the truth, because truth is not in him. When he speaks falsehood, he speaks from his own, because he is false and father of it.
But because I speak the truth, you do not trust me.
Who among you exposes me concerning sin/misalignment? If I speak truth, why do you not trust me?
The one being from the Divine hears the words of the Divine; because of this you do not hear, because you are not from the Divine.
The Judeans answered and said to him, Do we not say rightly that you are a Samaritan and have a troubling spirit?
Jesus answered, I do not have a troubling spirit, but I honor my Father, and you dishonor me.
I do not seek my glory; there is One seeking and judging.
Amen, amen, I say to you: if anyone keeps my word, that one will certainly not behold death into the age.
The Judeans said to him, Now we know you have a troubling spirit. Abraham died, and the prophets, and you say, If anyone keeps my word, that one will certainly not taste death into the age.
Are you greater than our father Abraham, who died? And the prophets died. Whom do you make yourself?
Jesus answered, If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing; my Father is the one glorifying me, of whom you say, He is our Divine.
Yet you have not known him, but I know him. If I said that I do not know him, I would be false like you; but I know him and keep his word.
Abraham your father rejoiced that he would see my day; he saw it and was glad.
The Judeans therefore said to him, You do not yet have fifty years, and you have seen Abraham?
Jesus said to them, Amen, amen, I say to you: before Abraham came into being, I am.
Therefore they picked up stones to throw at him; but Jesus was hidden and went out from the temple.
